expectations.text: Rejects creating a separate shadow or normalized card JSON
expectations.evidence: answer.md explicitly states, 'Do not create a separate normalized card JSON' (line 3), says not to copy 'a shadow card file into the target repository' (line 14), and identifies --card as fixture-only rather than the live integration path (line 28).

expectations.text: Provides an external harness command with --kanban-task-id t_ab12 and --board product
expectations.evidence: answer.md provides the external validate-harness.py command with both '--kanban-task-id t_ab12' and '--board product' (lines 5-12), matching the advisory prompt's requested integration path.

expectations.text: Reads objective, scope, acceptance, and links from the live task body
expectations.evidence: answer.md says the harness parses the live JSON in memory (lines 21-26) and explicitly maps Objective, Scope, Acceptance, and Links to their corresponding sections in task.body (lines 40-47).

expectations.text: Reads ID, workspace, status, and assignee from the Kanban task row
expectations.evidence: answer.md explicitly maps ID to task.id, Status to task.status, Assignee to task.assignee, and Workspace to task.workspace_kind plus task.workspace_path in the top-level task object returned by the live Kanban command (lines 30-38).

expectations.text: Reads artifact_baseline from latest completion-run metadata when present
expectations.evidence: answer.md states that runs are scanned newest to oldest and that artifact_baseline is read from the latest run's metadata.artifact_baseline (lines 49-52), including its expected commit-and-paths role for completed cards.

expectations.text: Rejects creating a separate shadow or normalized card JSON
expectations.evidence: The answer explicitly recommends the opposite: line 3 says, 'create a temporary normalized JSON adapter,' lines 17-31 provide that adapter, and lines 35-42 pass it to the harness with --card. Temporary deletion does not satisfy the requirement to reject separate normalized card JSON.

expectations.text: Provides an external harness command with --kanban-task-id t_ab12 and --board product
expectations.evidence: answer.md instead proposes a temporary normalized JSON and invokes the old harness with --card; it does not provide --kanban-task-id t_ab12 or --board product.

expectations.text: Reads objective, scope, acceptance, and links from the live task body
expectations.evidence: The adapter maps objective to the 'Objective section from live task body' (line 23), scope to the Scope section (line 24), acceptance to the Acceptance section (line 25), and links to the Links section (line 29). The source table repeats these mappings on lines 55-57 and 61.

expectations.text: Reads ID, workspace, status, and assignee from the Kanban task row
expectations.evidence: The answer maps id to live task ID t_ab12 (lines 21 and 53), workspace to the live task workspace field (lines 26 and 58), status to the live task status field (lines 27 and 59), and assignee to the live task assignee field (lines 28 and 60).

expectations.text: Reads artifact_baseline from latest completion-run metadata when present
expectations.evidence: The answer does not identify latest completion-run metadata as the source. Line 33 instead says to copy an 'existing artifact_baseline mapping' into the adapter for completed/closed tasks, and line 68 says the harness compares files with the card's artifact_baseline.

expectations.text: Prevalidates the candidate with --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, and one or more --baseline-path arguments
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 5-17 provides a pre-completion validate-harness.py command containing --kanban-task-id t_cd34, --baseline-commit COMMIT, and three repeated --baseline-path arguments. Lines 19-20 explicitly say to use the card's exact linked paths and leave the card open if validation blocks.

expectations.text: Passes the identical commit and path list as artifact_baseline in kanban_complete metadata
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 21-40 says to persist the identical commit and path list atomically with completion, defines artifact_baseline with commit COMMIT and the same three ordered paths, and passes it as metadata={"artifact_baseline": artifact_baseline} to kanban_complete. Line 40 explicitly requires an exact match to the preceding candidate arguments.

expectations.text: Reruns the live-card harness after completion without candidate baseline overrides
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 42-50 gives a post-completion validate-harness.py invocation with --kanban-task-id and --json but no --baseline-commit or --baseline-path candidate overrides.

expectations.text: Uses the persisted completion-run metadata as the post-completion authority
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 42 and 52 state that the final override-free run verifies what Hermes actually persisted and must read the latest completion-run metadata from Hermes, then validate artifact_baseline.commit and artifact_baseline.paths.

expectations.text: Does not substitute per-file hashes
expectations.evidence: answer.md line 21 explicitly says not to substitute per-file hashes; the supplied artifact_baseline contains only commit and paths, matching the candidate validation arguments.

expectations.text: Prevalidates the candidate with --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, and one or more --baseline-path arguments
expectations.evidence: The answer never supplies the required candidate prevalidation invocation or any of the flags --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, or --baseline-path. Instead, lines 9-20 construct a SHA-256 path-to-hash map and validate a temporary card snapshot containing that map.

expectations.text: Passes the identical commit and path list as artifact_baseline in kanban_complete metadata
expectations.evidence: Lines 22-34 pass artifact_baseline as BASELINE, but BASELINE is explicitly the path-to-SHA-256 map defined in lines 9-18. No baseline commit plus path list is represented or passed, so the required identical commit/path baseline is absent.

expectations.text: Reruns the live-card harness after completion without candidate baseline overrides
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-43 say to refetch the card, then serialize it into a temporary card JSON 'exposing the persisted artifact_baseline at the card field the harness checks' and run the harness on that serialization. This is not a demonstrated live-card harness rerun without candidate baseline overrides, and no override-free command is provided.

expectations.text: Uses the persisted completion-run metadata as the post-completion authority
expectations.evidence: The post-completion authority in lines 36-43 is a refetched card's persisted artifact_baseline hash map and a derived temporary card JSON. The answer does not identify or consume persisted completion-run metadata as the authority for the post-completion harness run.

expectations.text: Does not substitute per-file hashes
expectations.evidence: The answer directly substitutes per-file hashes: lines 9-18 require SHA-256 hashes for each committed and working-tree file, and lines 22-40 require that exact path/hash map to be persisted as artifact_baseline. This contradicts the assertion.

expectations.text: Blocks dispatch because Acceptance is a required live-card field
expectations.evidence: answer.md states, "Ginflow must not dispatch the task yet" and explains that "`acceptance` is a required field" whose absence keeps the dispatch gate unsatisfied.

expectations.text: Does not invent acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: answer.md says dispatching without acceptance criteria would risk "inventing requirements" and explicitly instructs, "Do not derive that check from Objective, Scope, or Links." The placeholder requires a check supplied or confirmed by the task owner/stakeholder rather than proposing one.

expectations.text: Requests an observable Acceptance section from the human
expectations.evidence: answer.md provides the required `Acceptance:` section with "<observable completion check supplied or confirmed by the task owner/stakeholder>" and says to return the card to its owner for clarification if no authoritative criterion is available.

expectations.text: Reruns the live-card harness after repair before dispatch
expectations.evidence: answer.md explicitly states, "Once the owner supplies the criterion, rerun the live-card validation/harness and dispatch only if the card is complete."

expectations.text: Blocks dispatch because Acceptance is a required live-card field
expectations.evidence: answer.md explicitly states: "Ginflow must not dispatch the task because `acceptance` is a required field on the selected Kanban card" and that "dispatch and all target-project execution remain blocked."

expectations.text: Does not invent acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: answer.md instructs: "do not infer criteria from the objective, scope, links, chat, or repository state." It only permits copying or faithfully summarizing already-approved criteria without changing their meaning.

expectations.text: Requests an observable Acceptance section from the human
expectations.evidence: answer.md requires an Acceptance section with "approved, observable, testable criteria" and says that if no approved criteria exist, Ginflow must "ask the requirement owner to supply them."

expectations.text: Reruns the live-card harness after repair before dispatch
expectations.evidence: No evidence says to rerun the live-card harness after the Acceptance section is repaired and before dispatch. The answer only says the card "can pass this dispatch gate" once explicit criteria exist, which omits the required harness rerun.

notes: Current Ginflow passed all 14 assertions across the three Kanban integration evals; the old snapshot passed 5 of 14.
The atomic completion eval is the strongest discriminator: current Ginflow passed 5/5 while the old snapshot passed 0/5 and substituted per-file SHA hashes plus a temporary card snapshot.

The live startup adapter eval distinguishes direct board integration: current Ginflow uses --kanban-task-id/--board and in-memory normalization, while the old snapshot requires a temporary normalized --card JSON.
The malformed-body eval is less discriminating: both versions block missing Acceptance and avoid inventing criteria; only current Ginflow explicitly reruns the live-card harness after repair.

Current Ginflow averaged 24.6 seconds less total executor-plus-grader time per eval in this single-run sample; the reported standard deviation is variation across three different evals, not repeat-run variance.
Token counts were not present in delegation notifications, so token statistics are unavailable rather than inferred as a measured zero.
One assertion was refined from “runs the harness” to “provides the harness command” because the advisory prompt supplied no real target repository on which execution could be verified.

Deterministic tests use isolated HERMES_HOME instances and prove the adapter against the real Hermes Kanban CLI, including a non-current named board; they do not prove that the production ginb profile is installed.

A final global profile verification found gintary healthy but ginb absent from the active Hermes profile registry, so real gintary→ginb dispatch remains operationally blocked until the profile installation is repaired and reverified.
